title: The External Activity Complex of a Pair of Matroids
speaker: Andrew Berget
abstract: I will introduce the external activity complex of an ordered pair of matroids on the same ground set. This is the combinatorial analogue of the Schubert variety of an ordered pair of linear subspaces of a fixed space, which is also new. These constructions generalize and extend the well-studied notion of external activity for a matroid basis, and the Schubert variety of a single linear space, which occur in the bivariate setting when one of the matroids is uniform of rank 1. Certain Betti numbers of the external activity complex of a pair determine the Euler characteristic of various (non-positive) tautological classes of matroids, and this can be used to give a positive formula for Speyer's matroid invariant $\(\omega(M)\)$. This is on-going work with Alex Fink.

proof_stages.label: Speyer face-vector motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk first frames David Speyer's conjectural upper bounds for internal faces in matroid subdivisions of the hypersimplex as one target consequence of the new construction.

proof_stages.label: Matroidal tautological bundles and Euler characteristics
proof_stages.summary: The second motivation is to compute or at least control signs and vanishing for Euler characteristics and higher cohomology of tensor products of exterior powers of matroidal quotient bundles on the permutohedral variety.

proof_stages.label: Diagonal Dilworth truncation of a matroid pair
proof_stages.summary: For a pair of matroids on the same ground set, the diagonal Dilworth truncation D(M1,M2) is defined via independent sets and circuits, with expected rank and circuit decompositions isolated as the structural data needed later.

proof_stages.label: Initial circuit decompositions and generalized external activity
proof_stages.summary: Choosing the lexicographically first decomposition of each circuit of D(M1,M2) defines when an element outside a basis is externally active, generalizing the classical matroid notion.

proof_stages.label: External activity complex
proof_stages.summary: The external activity complex is built with x- and y-vertices and facets indexed by bases of D(M1,M2), recording each basis together with its active and inactive outside elements.

proof_stages.label: Main theorem: Cohen-Macaulayness, bivaluativity, and K-polynomial formula
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts that the external activity complex is Cohen-Macaulay, that its finely graded K-polynomial is bivaluative, and that under expected rank its Z^2-graded K-polynomial generates the desired Euler characteristics with a controlled top-degree sign.

proof_stages.label: Extraction of the sign and cohomological refinement
proof_stages.summary: The sign statement is sketched by comparing the Zn-graded resolution of the external activity complex with Stanley's resolution of the independence complex of D, then translating extremal Betti numbers and Alexander duality into homology and cohomology formulas.

proof_stages.label: Geometric degeneration and tropical extension strategy
proof_stages.summary: The proof strategy is to study a Schubert-type variety of a pair of linear spaces that Gröbner degenerates to the external activity complex, then extend realizable results to all matroids using refined tropical intersection computations of Chern-class products.
